hard.next_step: 25th of June 2026 - New personal 'One 2 One' message sent to Marc Antoine to obtain more visibility on project status / next steps. RFP targeted September 2026, decision before end of calendar year.
hard.ais_why: Pre-RFI Avoma call on 2 Dec 2025 with Valerian de Peretti focused on traditional S2P digitalization goals (automating manual tasks, centralizing data, professionalisation). No AI-specific demand expressed by the buyer; the conversation is market scouting for a procurement platform broadly, not AI-first. Agentic AI is in product scope but not driving the buyer narrative.

north_star_verdict.headline: The 7 Mar on-site workshop landed strongly (buyer called Zycus the only vendor with a real intake-management solution), but the buyer has had no two-way exchange since, Pierre Meraud's 15 Jun and 25 Jun chases to Marc-Antoine Vannier are unanswered, the functional analysis promised for June is overdue, and the RFP has slipped from June to September 2026 with no champion, no named economic buyer, and an unnamed field of 10-15 RFI vendors.
north_star_verdict.math: 163 days to close from 2026-07-06. Deal created 2025-12-15, so 203 days old and in Qualified stage for its entire lifecycle (no stage progression in field history). Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 7 Mar 2026 on-site workshop, 121 days ago; every touch since (May event invites, 15 Jun and 25 Jun RFP-planning chases) is rep-initiated and unanswered. RFP has slipped April/May (Next Step 12 Feb) to June (Next Step 6 Jan) to September (Next Step 9 Apr, 15 Jun, 25 Jun); functional analysis due June is overdue. If the RFP only launches September, shortlist, vendor selection, negotiation and contracting must all complete in ~3.5 months to hit 16 Dec, so any further slip pushes the close into 2027.
evidence: Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was the 7 Mar 2026 on-site 2-hour workshop/demonstration, which drew extremely positive feedback and the buyer statement that Zycus is the only vendor offering a real intake-management solution (Next Step 16 Mar 2026).
Rep-side only since: Pierre Meraud sent RFP-planning chases on 15 Jun and a personal 'One 2 One' message to Marc-Antoine Vannier on 25 Jun 2026 (Confidence level 25%); no buyer reply is recorded.
Functional analysis the buyer promised to finalize by June (Next Step 9 Apr and 16 Mar) is overdue and unconfirmed.
RFP has slipped twice: April/May (Next Step 12 Feb) to June (Next Step 6 Jan) to September 2026 (Next Step 9 Apr, 15 Jun, 25 Jun).

No economic buyer engaged: the CPO holds budget authority but is reached only via DXC partner contact Zack and is 'really strict in terms of purchasing principle and ethic' (Next Step 16 Mar 2026); no Zycus-to-CPO contact recorded.
No champion developed: Marie Youssef (Responsable de la Performance Achats) was flagged as a potential champion on 16 Mar 2026 but shows no advocacy or engagement.
No compelling event: buyer said on 2 Dec 2025 this is 'professionalisation' and 'monter en maturite', not replacement of a failing tool; Next Step 16 Mar confirms 'No real compelling event'.
Deal is partner-led via DXC (Next Step 12 Feb 2026: 'Need to have DXC as Partner', call 13 Feb to confirm); Zycus has limited direct buyer access.

Competitive field unknown: on 2 Dec 2025 Valerian de Peretti said the RFI is a broad scan of 'une dizaine ou une quinzaine d'acteurs' (10-15 players, top editors plus niche specialists); no competitor is named in any field, task, or Next Step.
Products: iSource, eProcurement, Agentic AI (SF Products__c). AIS 4 (Moderate).

metrics.narrative: The pain is real and management-validated but the value case is entirely qualitative. On the 2 Dec 2025 call Valerian de Peretti named the drivers as 'le controle de la depense et le fait que les gens perdent beaucoup de temps dans les process achats' (spend control and people losing time in procurement processes), plus a want for more task automation, data centralization, a single source of truth and supplier-performance management. He noted a separate 'equipe de pilotage performance' is drafting the functional prerequisites and the ROI, but no cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure has been shared with Zycus in any call, task or field. With the CPO described as strict on purchasing and a September RFP ahead, quantifying savings with the performance team before the RFP is the highest-leverage metrics move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): 'le controle de la depense et le fait que les gens perdent beaucoup de temps dans les process achats'; 'un peu plus d'automatisation des taches, un peu plus de centralisation de la donnee, d'avoir une unique source de verite'
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'they know they have bad processess and almost no tools / project on the principle has been validated by management'

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget structure is known but no economic buyer is named or engaged. On 2 Dec 2025 Valerian de Peretti said the decision is transverse: 'une partie des DSI, il y aura la finance forcement pour tout ce qui est nomenclature, et la DSI interne pour l'integration, l'architecture', and that spend goes through a 'comite d'investissement' on the 2026 budget. Next Step 16 Mar 2026 identifies the CPO as the commercial authority, reachable via DXC partner contact Zack and 'really strict in terms of purchasing principle and ethic'. Valerian runs the group's centralized procurement function but is an evaluator, not the budget owner. No Salesforce contact role names the CPO, so the economic buyer stays role-level (CPO plus the investment committee and Finance); opening a Zycus-to-CPO path through DXC before the RFP is the move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): deciders are procurement, DSI/IT, Finance and an 'comite d'investissement'
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'direct contract with CPO but he is really strict in terms of purchasing principle and ethic' (access via DXC/Zack)
OpportunityContactRole: no Economic Buyer / Executive Sponsor role populated

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are emerging from the RFI conversation and the 7 Mar workshop, not yet formalized in a cahier des charges. On 2 Dec 2025 Valerian de Peretti framed the buyer's needs as task automation, data centralization, a single source of truth, productivity/efficiency gains and supplier-performance management, moving from an ERP-only stack with in-house tools. The strongest confirmed differentiator surfaced on 7 Mar: the buyer understood Zycus is the only vendor with a real intake-management solution (Next Step 16 Mar 2026), which is the wedge. AI is in scope but explicitly secondary: Valerian said on 2 Dec AI 'va etre en lien avec le RFI' rather than a primary demand. The formal criteria will crystallize in the September RFP, so shaping it now to embed intake management and the Merlin/Agentic AI story is decisive.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): automation, centralization, single source of truth, supplier performance
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'Prospect understood that we are the only one to offer a real intake management solution'

decision_process.narrative: The buyer laid out the process explicitly on 2 Dec 2025: a broad RFI to 10-15 vendors launched before Christmas 2025, a Q&A phase in January, demos late January, then shortlisting and a firmer cahier des charges, passage through an investment committee, and a two-stage RFP originally targeted June. This has since played out and slipped: RFI response sent 11 Feb, on-site workshop 7 Mar, and the RFP now targeted September 2026 with a decision before year-end (Next Step 9 Apr, 15 Jun, 25 Jun). The buying committee spans central procurement (Valerian de Peretti), IT (DSI) for integration and architecture, Finance, and an investment committee; the buyer may bring in external MOA/assistance for the RFP itself. The near-term gate is the buyer finalizing its functional analysis (overdue) before issuing the RFP.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): 'RFI... questions reponses debut janvier... demos fin janvier... comite d'investissement... appel d'offre en juin'
Next Step 12 Feb 2026: 'RFI response sent 11th of February, On site presentation 7th of March, RFP shortlisted April/May'
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'RFP to be sent in September for a decision before the end of this calendar year'

paper_process.narrative: The contracting mechanics are early and mostly unmapped, consistent with a Qualified-stage new-logo deal. An NDA has been signed (Next Step 6 Jan 2025: 'NDA Signed and send'), the first gating artifact, and DXC is intended as the implementation partner (Next Step 12 Feb 2026). There is no incumbent contract to displace (buyer runs ERP plus in-house tools only, no current source-to-pay platform per 2 Dec call), so there is no contract-expiration forcing function. No MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec or procurement-onboarding track has begun and none is expected until after the September RFP and vendor selection. Visibility will come from confirming DXC's partner role and mapping the investment-committee and procurement approval chain before the RFP.
sources: Next Step 6 Jan 2025: 'NDA Signed and send'
Next Step 12 Feb 2026: 'Need to have DXC as Partner (call on 13th of Feb to confirm)'
Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): 'A l'heure actuelle, on a juste un ERP... Pas de source to pay pour le moment'

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated and owned by procurement. On 2 Dec 2025 Valerian de Peretti said the two or three reasons for the project are spend control and time lost in manual procurement processes: contracts managed via internal tools and 'des jets qui se baladent', no supplier-performance steering, no single source of truth, and only an ERP with in-house applications rather than a source-to-pay platform. Docaposte is a digital company that builds its own applications but explicitly is not specialized in supply chain or procurement, so it is buying rather than building. The pain owner is the centralized group procurement function (Valerian and the performance-steering team); management has validated the project in principle (Next Step 16 Mar 2026), confirming the pain is recognized above the operational layer. What is not yet quantified is the financial impact.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): 'le controle de la depense et le fait que les gens perdent beaucoup de temps dans les process achats'; 'On a juste un ERP... pas de source to pay'
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'they know they have bad processess and almost no tools'

champion.narrative: No champion is developed and the engaged relationships sit at the operational layer. Valerian de Peretti (Lead IT Buyer and IT Procurement Specialist, PRIMARY Evaluator) ran the RFI conversation on 2 Dec 2025 and manages the group's centralized procurement, but he is an evaluator scouting the market, not an internal advocate driving Zycus. Marie Youssef (Responsable de la Performance Achats) was explicitly flagged on 16 Mar 2026 as the person to convert ('we need to see if we can transform Marie head of process and tools as a champion') and sits on the performance-steering team that owns the functional prerequisites and ROI, making her the natural champion, but there is no evidence she has been engaged. The only recent activity is Pierre Meraud's unanswered 25 Jun personal message to Marc-Antoine Vannier. There are no introductions to power, no shared internal documents, and no one pushing Zycus's case in the RFP drafting, which is the single biggest structural gap on the deal.
sources: Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'we need to see if we can transform Marie head of process and tools as a champion'
Next Step 25 Jun 2026: 'New personal One 2 One message sent to Marc Antoine to obtain more visibility on project status / next steps' (no reply)
OpportunityContactRole: Valerian de Peretti (Evaluator, PRIMARY), Marie Youssef (Responsable de la Performance Achats), Marc-Antoine Vannier, Julie Causse

competition.narrative: The field is competitive but entirely unnamed. On 2 Dec 2025 Valerian de Peretti described the RFI as a broad market scan of 'une dizaine ou une quinzaine d'acteurs' (10-15 players) spanning the top editors and niche specialists, so this is an open competitive process, not a sole-source. No specific rival is named in any Salesforce field (Competitors__c blank), Next Step, task or call, and Zycus has not seen the shortlist. The one confirmed edge is intake management: on 7 Mar the buyer said Zycus is the only vendor with a real intake-management solution (Next Step 16 Mar 2026). How we win: lock intake management plus the Merlin/Agentic AI story as differentiators in the September RFP criteria, and use a competitive-landscape conversation with Valerian to learn who else is in the frame before the RFP is written.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2 Dec 2025 (Valerian de Peretti): 'on brasse un peu large entre les tops editeurs de ce marche, puis regarder ceux qui sont un peu plus niches'; 'une dizaine ou une quinzaine d'acteurs'
Next Step 16 Mar 2026: 'we are the only one to offer a real intake management solution'
SF Competitors__c: not populated

deal_movement.summary: Deal peaked at the 7 Mar 2026 on-site workshop (extremely positive, intake-management wedge confirmed), then cooled: the buyer went into internal functional-analysis mode, the RFP slipped to September, and rep-side chases on 15 and 25 Jun have gone unanswered.

Close date pushed from 18 Nov 2026 to 16 Dec 2026

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source, so there is no current preferred rival to rank; the read is an OPEN competitive RFI of 10-15 vendors (Valerian de Peretti, 2 Dec 2025) in which Zycus holds one confirmed edge, intake management (buyer, 7 Mar 2026), and no visibility into the rest of the field. The real threat is not a named rival but a written RFP that Zycus did not help shape plus buyer inertia (no compelling event). Win path: embed intake management and the Merlin/Agentic AI narrative in the September RFP criteria and extract the shortlist from Valerian before it is finalized.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong functional fit on the buyer's stated needs (automation, centralization, intake management), with AI and IT integration as the exposed edges the RFP could turn into criteria.

best_practice_check.summary: Three levers decide this deal now: multi-thread beyond a silent single contact, shape the RFP before it is written, and manufacture urgency where there is no compelling event.
flags: Multi-thread off the silent thread: Pierre Meraud's chases to Marc-Antoine Vannier are unanswered, so re-anchor on Valerian de Peretti (primary evaluator who ran the RFI) and Marie Youssef (performance/ROI team).
Shape the September RFP before it is drafted: get Zycus's intake-management and Merlin/Agentic AI differentiators embedded in the cahier des charges rather than responding to someone else's criteria.
No compelling event: tie the project to Docaposte's stated 2026 budget and investment-committee cycle to create a forcing clock, or the deal drifts into 2027.

critical_signals.text: The 7 Mar on-site workshop landed strongly and the buyer said Zycus is the only vendor with a real intake-management solution, which is our clearest wedge.
This is an open RFI of 10-15 vendors that Valerian described on 2 Dec, and Zycus has no names and no view of the shortlist.
Marie Youssef of the performance and ROI team is the untapped champion path flagged on 16 Mar but has never been engaged.
The $300k was set in February but no ROI or savings figure is quantified and the CPO who owns budget is described as strict on purchasing.

champion_strength.summary: No champion is developed. Marie Youssef (Responsable de la Performance Achats), who owns the functional prerequisites and ROI, was flagged on 16 Mar 2026 as the person to convert but has never been engaged. Valerian de Peretti is an open, cooperative evaluator from the 2 Dec RFI call but scouts the market rather than advocating internally, and the current thread, Marc-Antoine Vannier, has gone silent to the 15 and 25 Jun chases. The deal is single-threaded through operational procurement with no internal advocate driving the RFP or opening a path to the CPO.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI is in scope but under-positioned: the buyer's narrative is automation and centralization, and on 2 Dec AI was only 'en lien avec le RFI', so Zycus has not yet made Merlin/Agentic AI a differentiator. The September RFP is the window to put the AI intake-and-sourcing story in front of the buyer and shape the AI criteria.

Appetite is present but not driving, and the RFP has not yet mandated AI evaluation.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We landed the 7 Mar workshop as the only vendor with a real intake-management solution, but the buyer has gone quiet for four months, the RFP slipped from June to September, and we hold no champion, no named economic buyer, and zero visibility into the 10-15 vendors on the RFI.

win_position.text: Zycus is early but differentiated: Valerian de Peretti ran a broad RFI market scan on 2 Dec, and after the 7 Mar on-site workshop the buyer called Zycus the only vendor with a real intake-management solution, our clearest wedge into this France procurement-transformation deal.

The position is fragile because the buyer has had no two-way exchange since 7 Mar and Pierre Meraud's 15 Jun and 25 Jun chases to Marc-Antoine Vannier went unanswered, so our only preference signal is now four months stale.
No economic buyer is engaged: the CPO who holds budget is reached only through DXC's Zack and is described as strict on purchasing (16 Mar), and the field of 10-15 RFI vendors is entirely unnamed.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has thinned: the RFP slipped twice (April/May to June to September per Next Step 12 Feb and 9 Apr) and the functional analysis the buyer promised for June is now overdue with no confirmation.
No buyer-initiated meeting or dated commitment has landed in the last 60 days; the only recent activity is rep-side email on 25 Jun awaiting a reply.

customer_commitment.text: The project is validated in principle by Docaposte management but carries no compelling event, since the buyer said on 2 Dec this is professionalisation and maturity, not replacement of a failing tool.
Commercially exposed: $300k was set 12 Feb but no ROI or savings figure is quantified and the CPO is described as strict on purchasing.

deal_risk.text: Single-threaded and partner-dependent: engagement runs through operational procurement (Valerian, Marc-Antoine) with no executive access, and the CPO relationship is mediated entirely by DXC's Zack.
Timeline risk to the 16 Dec close: a September RFP forces shortlist, selection, negotiation and contracting into ~3.5 months, so any further slip pushes the close into 2027.
